data.title: Amazon expects Q2 net sales up 16%-19%
data.description:
Overview

  • E-commerce and cloud giant's Q1 net sales rose 17%, beat analyst expectations

  • EPS for Q1 rose 75% yr/yr

  • Free cash flow dropped due to higher AI-related capital investments

Outlook

  • Amazon expects Q2 2026 net sales between $194.0 bln and $199.0 bln, up 16%-19% y/y

  • Company sees Q2 2026 operating income of $20.0 bln to $24.0 bln vs $19.2 bln y/y

  • Guidance anticipates unfavorable impact of about 10 basis points from foreign exchange rates

Result Drivers

  • AWS GROWTH - AWS segment sales rose 28% yr/yr, the fastest growth in 15 quarters, per CEO Andy Jassy

  • AI-RELATED INVESTMENTS - Free cash flow fell due to a $59.3 bln yr/yr increase in property and equipment purchases, mainly reflecting investments in artificial intelligence

  • ANTHROPIC INVESTMENT GAINS - Net income included $16.8 bln in pre-tax gains from investments in Anthropic

data.title: TTM Technologies Q1 revenue jumps 30%, beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US circuit board maker's Q1 revenue rose 30% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Non-GAAP net income for Q1 hit a record as Data Center and Networking demand remained strong

  • Aerospace & Defense and Medical, Industrial, and Instrumentation end markets posted double-digit growth

Outlook

  • TTM sees Q2 net sales between $930 mln and $970 mln

  • Company expects Q2 non-GAAP EPS of $0.82 to $0.88

  • TTM expects first-half 2026 growth pace to continue in second half

Result Drivers

  • DATA CENTER & NETWORKING DEMAND - Co said continued strong demand in Data Center and Networking, driven by AI and data center buildouts, boosted Q1 revenue

  • MEDICAL, INDUSTRIAL & INSTRUMENTATION GROWTH - Co reported strong double-digit yr/yr revenue growth in Medical, Industrial and Instrumentation end market

  • AEROSPACE & DEFENSE GROWTH - Co said Aerospace and Defense end market grew double-digits yr/yr, above expectations

data.title: FormFactor Q1 revenue slightly beats estimates, raises Q2 outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • Semiconductor test equipment maker's fiscal Q1 revenue rose 32% yr/yr, slightly beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted net income for fiscal Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company cites record DRAM revenue and strong demand for probe cards in networking

Outlook

  • FormFactor sees Q2 revenue of $240 mln +/- $5 mln

  • Company expects Q2 non-GAAP gross margin of 49.5% +/- 1.5%

  • FormFactor forecasts Q2 non-GAAP EPS of $0.61 +/- $0.04

Result Drivers

  • DRAM DEMAND - Co reported record DRAM revenue, citing increased demand in HBM applications and sustained demand in non-HBM applications

  • NETWORKING APPLICATIONS - Significant increase in Foundry & Logic demand driven by growth in probe cards for networking applications

  • RECORD REVENUE - All-time record revenue attributed to strong demand in key end markets, per CEO Mike Slessor

data.title: Norway oil firms seek mediation to avert labour strike that could hit output
data.description:
Wage talks between Norwegian oil firms and labour unions broke down on Wednesday and will switch to state-led mediation in a bid to prevent strike action by workers, industry group Offshore Norge said.

If Norway's government-appointed mediator is unable to broker a deal when negotiations resume later this year, union members will be eligible to go on strike, disrupting output from western Europe's largest oil and gas producer.

Unions Styrke, Safe and Lederne failed to reach an agreement with the companies, Offshore Norge said.

While no date has yet been set, oil companies have said they expect mediation to take place in June.

Norway produces around 4 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, almost equally divided between oil and natural gas, and any production cuts could impact markets at a time when Middle East output is also severely curtailed.

The unions had been negotiating since Tuesday with Offshore Norway, which represents petroleum companies, in a bid to agree an offshore wage settlement for about 8,000 workers.

The talks cover wages, benefits and working conditions for staff employed by oil firms, including Equinor , Aker BP , ConocoPhillips and Vaar Energi .

data.title: eBay gives upbeat 2026 revenue outlook after Q1 beat
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. online marketplace's Q1 revenue rose 19%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations, up 21% year-over-year

  • Company repurchased $500 mln in shares and paid $139 mln in dividends in Q1

Outlook

  • eBay sees Q2 2026 revenue between $2.97 bln and $3.03 bln

  • Company expects Q2 2026 GMV between $21.3 bln and $21.7 bln

  • eBay forecasts Q2 2026 diluted GAAP EPS of $1.09 to $1.14

Result Drivers

  • FOCUS CATEGORIES & C2C - Co said growth in Focus Categories and consumer-to-consumer (C2C) initiatives drove broad-based momentum

  • ADVERTISING GROWTH - First-party advertising revenue rose 33% year-over-year, contributing to overall revenue growth

  • PLATFORM ENHANCEMENTS - Expansion of eBay Live and new AI-powered features increased buyer engagement and seller reach

data.title: Woodward's Q2 sales beat, outlook raised
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. aerospace and industrial controls maker's fiscal Q2 sales rose 23%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal Q2 rose 34% year-over-year

  • Company raised full-year sales and adjusted EPS guidance on continued demand strength

Outlook

  • Woodward raises FY26 sales growth guidance to 20%-23% from 14%-18%

  • Company lifts FY26 adjusted EPS forecast to $9.15-$9.45 from $8.20-$8.60

  • Woodward expects FY26 Aerospace sales growth of 21%-24% and Industrial sales growth of 18%-20%

Result Drivers

  • AEROSPACE DEMAND - Co said Aerospace performance was driven by continued strength in commercial services activity and OEM demand

  • INDUSTRIAL GROWTH - Co said Industrial grew across transportation, power generation, and oil and gas

  • PRICE REALIZATION - Co said higher segment earnings were due in part to price realization, partially offset by inflation and increased investments

data.title: Aware Q1 revenue falls, net loss widens on higher expenses
data.description:
Overview

  • Biometric identity solutions firm's Q1 revenue declined yr/yr due to lower perpetual license sales

  • Net loss widened in Q1, reflecting higher operating expenses and one-time severance costs

  • Company expects $4 mln annualized reduction in operating expenses starting Q2 2026 after restructuring

Outlook

  • Aware expects to reduce annualized operating expenses by $4.0 mln starting in Q2 2026

  • Company says it will continue to adjust operating expenses to align with strategic goals

  • Aware sees growing demand for biometric orchestration from government and enterprise markets

Result Drivers

  • REVENUE MIX SHIFT - Co said Q1 revenue decline was primarily due to lower perpetual license revenue, partially offset by higher subscription license revenue

  • RESTRUCTURING COSTS - Higher operating expenses in Q1 were driven by one-time severance costs related to workforce reduction and increased compensation from prior-year hires

  • STRATEGIC TRANSITION - Co said it is shifting to a platform-first approach, focusing on biometric orchestration for government and enterprise markets
